header.title: HealthMonitor, in detail
header.intro: A complete look at how the platform turns your lab analyses into useful information: the eight monitored conditions, the 26 analyzed markers, the verified specialist ecosystem, the technology behind it, and the cryptographic guarantees that protect your data.

conditions.title: Eight conditions. One single risk profile.
conditions.subtitle: The platform simultaneously monitors the eight disease groups with the highest impact on adult population health. Every prediction is trained on public CDC datasets and validated against standard clinical metrics.

cardiovascular.name: Cardiovascular Risk
cardiovascular.description: Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of death globally, and also the most preventable. Your risk is computed via the Framingham 2008 score — the same one used by cardiologists worldwide — and captures the probability of a major cardiovascular event in the next 10 years. Early detection turns a predisposition into a risk you can manage through lifestyle change.
cardiovascular.stat: 32% of global deaths are caused by cardiovascular disease (WHO, 2021).

diabetes.name: Diabetes
diabetes.description: Type 2 diabetes develops slowly — often with years of asymptomatic 'prediabetes' before a diagnosis. Our model classifies you into three categories — healthy, borderline (prediabetes), and diabetic — to surface signals before the condition becomes irreversible. In early stages, reversibility is possible through dietary change.
diabetes.stat: 1 in 10 adults globally has diabetes; nearly half don't know it (IDF Diabetes Atlas, 2021).

metabolicSyndrome.name: Metabolic Syndrome
metabolicSyndrome.description: Metabolic syndrome is a cluster of five factors — increased waist circumference, elevated glucose, high triglycerides, low HDL and high blood pressure — that together double cardiovascular risk. Diagnosis is made when at least three of five coexist, per the IDF / ATP III criteria.
metabolicSyndrome.stat: Metabolic syndrome doubles the risk of cardiovascular events and increases all-cause mortality by over 50% (Mottillo et al., meta-analysis of 951,083 patients, 2010).

hepaticSteatosis.name: Hepatic Steatosis
hepaticSteatosis.description: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) is the accumulation of fat in the liver and often appears in people with normal or slightly elevated weight. In early stages it is completely asymptomatic and reversible, but untreated it can progress to inflammation, fibrosis and cirrhosis. Our model uses the Fatty Liver Index (FLI), clinically validated by Bedogni et al., 2006.
hepaticSteatosis.stat: 1 in 4 adults globally has NAFLD, but most are undiagnosed (Younossi et al., 2019).

ckd.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
ckd.description: CKD is the progressive loss of kidney function and is one of the most silent conditions — over 80% of those affected don't know they have it. Our model calculates the glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) using the CKD-EPI 2021 formula, currently the most accurate in clinical use. Early intervention can significantly slow progression.
ckd.stat: 9 in 10 adults with chronic kidney disease are undiagnosed (CDC, 2023).

anemia.name: Anemia
anemia.description: Anemia means fewer red blood cells or low hemoglobin — and therefore less oxygen delivered to tissues. Causes vary: iron deficiency, vitamin shortages, chronic blood loss, or hidden pathology. Our model applies the WHO 2011 thresholds, differentiated by sex, for an accurate diagnosis.
anemia.stat: Approximately 1.8 billion people have anemia globally — over a quarter of the population (WHO, 2021).

hypertension.name: Hypertension
hypertension.description: Hypertension is the 'silent killer' — often without symptoms, but causing stroke, heart attack and kidney failure. Only half of those affected are diagnosed, and only a quarter have controlled blood pressure. Early detection changes the entire trajectory of the disease.
hypertension.stat: 1.28 billion adults globally have hypertension; 46% don't know they have it (WHO, 2023).

dyslipidemia.name: Dyslipidemia
dyslipidemia.description: Dyslipidemia is the imbalance of blood lipids — high LDL cholesterol, elevated triglycerides, or low HDL. It is the central factor in the atherogenesis that leads to heart attack and stroke, but responds exceptionally well to dietary change and to statins when indicated. Early detection is key.
dyslipidemia.stat: 39% of adults globally have elevated total cholesterol (WHO, 2023).

markers.title: 26 tests. One complete picture.
markers.subtitle: All values are automatically extracted from your lab PDF — no manual entry. The 26 tests are organized into six clinical groups and cover the complete metabolic, cardiovascular, hepatic, renal and hematological spectrum in a complementary fashion.

groupCardiovascular.title: Cardiovascular
groupCardiovascular.description: How your heart and blood vessels function. Blood pressure, fractioned cholesterol, triglycerides and systemic inflammation (CRP) predict the risk of major cardiovascular events over a 10-year horizon.

groupMetabolic.title: Metabolic
groupMetabolic.description: How you process sugar, store energy and maintain hormonal balance. Glucose, HbA1c, waist circumference, UACR and vitamin D describe your long-term metabolic health.

groupHepatic.title: Hepatic
groupHepatic.description: Liver health — the organ that metabolizes everything you eat, drink and breathe. ALT, AST and GGT are the enzymes that, when elevated, signal hepatic inflammation or fat accumulation.

groupRenal.title: Renal
groupRenal.description: Kidney function — the ability to clear waste products from your blood. Creatinine, urea and uric acid are the standardized indicators of glomerular filtration.

groupHematological.title: Hematological
groupHematological.description: Your blood in detail — red cells, iron and essential nutrients. Hemoglobin, MCV, ferritin and folate together describe oxygen-carrying capacity and hidden nutritional deficiencies.

groupUniversal.title: General Profile
groupUniversal.description: The data that contextualizes everything else — age, biological sex and body mass index. Visible to all the specialists you work with and calibrated into every AI model.

markers.extractionNote: Marker names shown in the cards come from the Synevo, Regina Maria and MedLife reporting standards.

specialists.title: Verified specialists. Clear roles.
specialists.subtitle: Every specialist is manually verified by our team before they can interact with patients — through a transparent process similar to the one used by the Romanian Medical College.
specialists.verificationIntro: The verification process varies by role: doctors are validated through the parafa code issued by the Romanian Medical College (CMR), nutritionists through their ONDR registration number, and personal trainers through ANEFS, NASM, ACE or ISSA certifications. All documents are reviewed individually by an administrator before an account is activated.

cardiologie.name: Cardiology
cardiologie.description: The cardiologist monitors blood pressure, fractioned cholesterol, triglycerides, CRP and smoking status — all the factors that predict major cardiovascular events.

endocrinologie.name: Endocrinology
endocrinologie.description: The endocrinologist tracks the complete metabolic profile — glucose, HbA1c, lipids, vitamin D, waist circumference — to detect diabetes, hormonal imbalances and metabolic syndrome.

diabetNutritie.name: Diabetes, Nutrition & Metabolic Diseases
diabetNutritie.description: The diabetes, nutrition and metabolic disease specialist is the meeting point between endocrinology and nutrition — coordinating therapeutic and nutritional plans for patients with prediabetes, diabetes and metabolic syndrome.

gastroenterologie.name: Gastroenterology
gastroenterologie.description: The gastroenterologist interprets liver enzymes (ALT, AST, GGT), triglycerides and ferritin to evaluate hepatic and digestive tract health.

hepatologie.name: Hepatology
hepatologie.description: The hepatologist focuses exclusively on the liver — managing hepatic steatosis, fibrosis and the evolution of ALT, AST, GGT, plus ferritin and UACR when associated metabolic pathology is present.

nefrologie.name: Nephrology
nefrologie.description: The nephrologist oversees renal function via creatinine, urea, uric acid, UACR and hemoglobin — calibrating treatment when the glomerular filtration rate declines.

hematologie.name: Hematology
hematologie.description: The hematologist interprets hemoglobin, MCV, ferritin, folate and CRP to diagnose anemia, nutritional deficiencies and systemic inflammation.

urologie.name: Urology
urologie.description: The urologist has access to renal function (creatinine, urea, uric acid) and UACR to evaluate urinary tract pathology and monitor infections or kidney stones.

medicinaInterna.name: Internal Medicine
medicinaInterna.description: The internist has the broadest perspective — they see all 26 markers by default and act as a coordination point between organ specialties.

nutritionist.name: Nutritionist
nutritionist.description: The nutritionist accesses the complete metabolic and lipid profile — to build personalized meal plans that reflect biological reality, not just preferences.

antrenor.name: Personal Trainer
antrenor.description: The personal trainer sees waist circumference and general vital parameters to calibrate training programs adapted to the trainee's cardiovascular profile.

technology.subtitle: Four technical pillars that make the platform unique: per-marker encryption, clinically-weighted scoring, mirror comparison graphs and the real-time ecosystem.
technology.abeDesc: Each of the 26 markers is encrypted individually under a mathematical policy that reflects the medical specializations that need it. Concretely: a cardiologist connected to your profile sees your cholesterol, triglycerides and CRP — but your kidney values remain mathematically inaccessible to their key. The CP-ABE scheme described by Bethencourt, Sahai and Waters (2007).
technology.scoreDesc: Not a flat average — cardiovascular risk weighs the most in the score because it is the leading cause of mortality. Each condition is weighted by clinical impact, following the logic of the Charlson Comorbidity Index (Charlson et al., 1987).
technology.mirrorDesc: Compare your June results with today's. See the exact trajectory of each marker over time and instantly identify whether your interventions — diet, exercise, medication — are actually working.
technology.ecoDesc: When the A.I. detects a critical threshold — glucose above 250, HbA1c above 9.5 — your connected specialists receive an instant alert in their dashboard, even when they're working in a different tab.
technology.chatMsg1: Glucose is down! Great progress on the new diet.
technology.chatMsg2: Thanks! Shall we keep the plan?

security.subtitle: The difference between a promise that your data is safe and a mathematical guarantee that cannot be cheated.
security.block1Body: Traditional medical platforms store your data in a database and control access through software rules. A misconfigured permission, a compromised administrator account, or a legal subpoena can expose all your records. The data is visible — security is just a locked door.
security.block2Body: With Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption, each medical value becomes unreadable text without the correct mathematical key. The key is indissolubly bound to the doctor's role (for example, the 'cardiologist' specialization) and their specific relationship with you as a patient. Neither the platform administrator, nor an attacker with full database access, can read the data without a valid key.
security.block3Intro: Here's what happens when your cardiologist needs to see your kidney markers:
security.block3Step1: The doctor sends a request listing the markers they need and a written justification (between 20 and 1000 characters).
security.block3Step2: You receive the request directly in the platform's chat and read the concrete reason — not a generic prompt.
security.block3Step3: You approve the specific markers you want to unlock — check one, two, or ten — exactly the number you choose.
security.block3Step4: The system re-issues an updated cryptographic key, valid ONLY for your profile and ONLY for the markers you approved.
security.block3Step5: If you revoke the relationship with the doctor, their key becomes instantly invalid — with no further action from you. The markers become mathematically inaccessible again.
security.block4Row2HealthMonitor: Data remains encrypted, useless without the key
security.block4Row3HealthMonitor: Per individual marker (up to 26 distinct decisions)

mlAccuracy.subtitle: AI models are powerful tools only when trained on accurate, representative and publicly verifiable data.
mlAccuracy.nhanesBody: The National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey is the US population health surveillance program run by the CDC (Centers for Disease Control and Prevention). For over 60 years, NHANES has measured clinical, demographic and nutritional parameters on representative samples — fully public, reproducible data. Our models use four consecutive cycles (2013–2014, 2015–2016, 2017–2018, 2019–2020) and approximately 11,400 adult patients with complete profiles across 26 markers.
mlAccuracy.modelBTitle: Why we exclude the diagnostic marker
mlAccuracy.modelBBody: An A.I. model that sees the HbA1c value and predicts diabetes is clinically irrelevant — any physician would reach the same diagnosis from the same data. Our production model is trained without seeing the marker that defines the disease — it learns from adjacent risk factors: weight, age, lipid profile, vitamin D, lifestyle. This is real early detection — we identify the risk before the diagnostic marker itself changes. The accuracy below is on Model B, the honest model.
mlAccuracy.treesNote: For tabular clinical data, tree-based models (XGBoost, Random Forest) still outperform deep neural networks (Grinsztajn et al., NeurIPS 2022). That informs our technical choice.
mlAccuracy.accuracyTitle: Model performance — AUC-ROC on Model B
mlAccuracy.accuracyDiabetesNote: Diabetes — F1-macro 0.63 (three-class model: Healthy / Borderline / Diabetic; different metric from binary AUC)
mlAccuracy.disclaimerBody: HealthMonitor is a Clinical Decision Support tool, designed to help you understand your tests and support your conversations with your specialist. It is NOT a diagnostic tool. The results generated by AI algorithms are advisory and do NOT replace licensed medical consultation, clinical diagnosis, or treatment prescribed by a physician. In accordance with Romanian Law no. 46/2003 on patient rights, therapeutic decisions remain exclusively the responsibility of your treating physician.

hero.subtitle: We automatically extract values from your lab PDFs, run them through 8 AI models trained on <1>11,400 real patients</1>, and deliver them directly to your doctor, nutritionist or coach — in a single platform encrypted field by field.
hero.poweredBy: Trained on NHANES 2013–2020 · CDC public data · 11,400 patients · 26 clinical markers

howItWorks.sectionSubtitle: Three steps — from the PDF on your desk to a personalized plan you can follow.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Drop in your PDF from Synevo, Regina Maria, MedLife or any other lab. The system reads the tables on its own, identifies all 26 clinical markers and maps them into your profile. If a test is missing, you fill it in through a form that mirrors your printed report. No retyping dozens of numbers, no transcription errors.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Eight AI models (XGBoost and Random Forest) evaluate your risk in parallel across eight major condition groups — cardiovascular, metabolic, hepatic, renal, hematological. Missing values are statistically estimated via MICE imputation, the same method used in landmark epidemiological studies. All predictions converge into a single clinically-weighted Health Score — not a flat average, but a sum where cardiovascular risk carries the heaviest weight.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Connect with your doctor, nutritionist or coach — all manually verified via Romanian Medical College parafa code, ONDR registry number, or sports certifications. Real-time chat brings your specialist one click away. When the A.I. detects a critical signal — glucose above 250, HbA1c above 9.5 — your specialist gets an instant alert, even if they're on a different page. Get personalized plans delivered straight into the platform.

mlAccuracyTeaser.title: Validated on real data, not on promises.
mlAccuracyTeaser.disclaimerShort: Range across 7 conditions. Validated on NHANES.

cta.title: Two paths, one goal.
cta.patientBody: Upload your first lab PDF in under a minute. Instantly receive a clinically-weighted Health Score, eight A.I. predictions and a historical chart of every marker. All data stays mathematically encrypted — even we, as the operator, cannot read your values without your authorization.
cta.specialistBody: Sign up with your CMR parafa code, ONDR registration number or coach certification. After our administrator verifies your documents, you gain access to records of patients who have invited you — exactly to the markers relevant for your specialization. Request extended access through chat when there is a real clinical need.
